tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent crashed to ~$97/bbl on the Apr 8 ceasefire (-18%) but ceasefire is conditional and Iran claims Hormuz still closed (Israeli Lebanon strikes). Re-enter on dips toward $90-93 if ceasefire holds through Vance-Pakistan talks Apr 12 and transit volumes recover. If ceasefire collapses, prior tail range $140-185/bbl is back in play. PTTEP and Medco benefit less at $97 than at $118 but still outperform ASEAN importers. Reduce position size; hold options overlay. Conviction medium pending Apr 12 outcome.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent at ~$97 and iTraxx Asia IG at ~178bp reflect ceasefire relief but the ceasefire is fragile. ASEAN Energy Equities fell -15.7% on the oil crash; ASEAN Banks recovered +4.5% as spreads narrowed. Partially trim the energy long and reduce the bank underweight - iTraxx at 178bp is approaching the 170bp trigger. Maintain a residual ASEAN Energy overweight against banks in case the ceasefire collapses within the two-week window. Wait for Vance-Pakistan talks Apr 12 before full position unwind.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short - Dip Opportunity on Trump-Driven Rally
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP has strengthened to ~57.90 (est.) on ceasefire relief, moving against this position significantly. Brent at ~$97 and JKM at ~$19.50 reduce the Philippine import bill materially. Exit or significantly reduce short PHP exposure; prior 61.00-62.00 stress target is not realistic under the ceasefire scenario. Retain a small hedge via options in case ceasefire collapses within 2 weeks. Reinstate full short on confirmed Hormuz re-closure or ceasefire breakdown.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short - Re-Entry on Trump Pause Dip
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR has eased to ~16,600 (est.) and Indonesia 10Y to ~7.18% (est.) as the ceasefire reverses risk-off positioning. Brent at ~$97 closes the ICP gap to ~$27/bbl, significantly reducing fiscal stress vs. the $48/bbl overshoot at peak. Exit short IDR position; the confirmed ceasefire plus oil retracement stop-loss condition has been met. Reinstate on ceasefire collapse or if Brent re-exceeds $105. Hold IDR bond underweight at smaller size pending Vance-Pakistan Apr 12 outcome.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection - Hold After Deadline
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia IG narrowed to ~178bp (-28bp) and ASEAN HY to ~545bp (-47bp) on ceasefire relief. Reduce CDS protection to a core hedge size; the immediate crisis premium is partially unwinding. Do not fully unwind: ceasefire is conditional, Iran claims Hormuz still closed due to Lebanon, and Saudi East-West Pipeline attack confirms Iran retains offensive capacity. 160-170bp is the next support if Vance talks succeed; re-add on any spread widening above 195bp.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF - Hold Into Phase Two
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM has eased to ~$19.50/MMBtu (est.) and TTF to ~€45.50/MWh on ceasefire relief, with Asia relative scarcity partially unwinding. Reduce JKM long size; the prior $24-28/MMBtu target is not realistic under a ceasefire scenario. Iran's Hormuz closure claim and Ras Laffan force majeure mean JKM-TTF spread stays positive. Hold a residual JKM long for ceasefire-collapse optionality; JKM re-scarcity target $22-25/MMBtu if Hormuz formally re-closes. Stop on confirmed Ras Laffan restart.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: Ceasefire partially reopens the window: iTraxx Asia IG has narrowed to ~178bp and ASEAN HY to ~545bp. Act immediately on the window - it is conditional on the 2-week ceasefire holding and Vance-Pakistan talks (Apr 12) succeeding. Execute secured bank lines and DFI-backed structures now; a ceasefire collapse would re-widen spreads sharply. Do not wait for full normalization.

PTTEP: ceasefire improves the THB window - TH 10Y eased to ~2.22% and USD/THB strengthened to ~33.20 (est.). Brent at ~$97 reduces upstream EBITDA vs. peak but remains materially above the pre-shock $65 level. Execute H2 2026 USD prefunding now; the 2-week ceasefire window is real but fragile. Gulf JV sanctions-compliance review remains urgent given uncertain Hormuz normalization.

Ceasefire creates a narrow execution window: iTraxx Asia IG has narrowed to ~178bp and USD/SGD has strengthened to ~1.2780 (est.). Brent at ~$97 and JKM at ~$19.50 reduce working capital pressure vs. peak. Execute revolving-credit expansion now while the window is open - it is conditional on the ceasefire holding through Apr 12 Vance talks. Maintain USD hedging; ceasefire collapse risk is 30%.

Ceasefire relief: ASEAN HY has narrowed to ~545bp and Brent to ~$97. However, Hyflux's utility stress is structural - Kuwait desalination precedent is still live, Hormuz is claimed closed by Iran, and ceasefire is only 2-week conditional. Use the spread-tightening window to execute private-credit and quasi-sovereign restructuring now; do not treat the ceasefire as full resolution. Collapse risk 30% within 2 weeks.
